questions.q: What is the primary difference between benign and malignant tumors?
options: Malignant tumors are always painful
Benign tumors do not invade surrounding tissues or metastasize
Benign tumors grow faster than malignant ones
Malignant tumors are always visible on imaging
questions.explanation: Benign tumors remain localized and do not invade surrounding tissues or spread to distant sites. Malignant tumors have the capacity to invade and metastasize, though pain is not a defining characteristic of either.

questions.q: Which pathogen is responsible for causing tuberculosis?
options: Mycobacterium tuberculosis
Streptococcus pneumoniae
Treponema pallidum
Vibrio cholerae
questions.explanation: Mycobacterium tuberculosis is the bacterium causing tuberculosis, spread primarily through respiratory droplets. Streptococcus pneumoniae causes pneumonia, not TB.

questions.q: Inflammation is characterized by which cardinal signs?
options: Pain, heat, and swelling only
Redness, heat, swelling, pain, and loss of function
Swelling and fever exclusively
Redness and pain only
questions.explanation: Celsus described the five cardinal signs of inflammation: calor (heat), rubor (redness), tumor (swelling), dolor (pain), and functio laesa (loss of function). All five together characterize true inflammation.

questions.q: Type 2 diabetes primarily results from what physiological dysfunction?
options: Failure of the pancreas to produce any insulin
Insulin resistance and relative insulin deficiency
Complete autoimmune destruction of beta cells
Inability of the liver to store glucose
questions.explanation: Type 2 diabetes involves insulin resistance where cells do not respond adequately to insulin, combined with insufficient insulin production. Type 1 involves autoimmune beta cell destruction, not Type 2.

questions.q: Which of the following is characteristic of chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD)?
options: Acute onset with full recovery possible
Reversible airway obstruction similar to asthma
Irreversible or poorly reversible airway obstruction
Purely genetic condition without environmental factors
questions.explanation: COPD involves irreversible or poorly reversible airway obstruction, primarily from smoking. Unlike asthma, the obstruction is largely fixed, though some reversibility may occur with treatment.

questions.q: Atherosclerosis occurs when lipid-rich plaques accumulate in arteries. What is the primary component of these plaques?
options: Protein aggregates
Cholesterol, lipids, and fibrous tissue
Calcium deposits exclusively
Bacterial biofilms
questions.explanation: Atherosclerotic plaques consist of lipids (particularly cholesterol), smooth muscle cells, and fibrous tissue forming a core covered by a fibrous cap. Calcium is deposited in advanced plaques but is not the primary component.

questions.q: What is the primary cause of hypertension in approximately 90-95% of cases?
options: Kidney disease
Endocrine disorders
Primary (essential) hypertension
Valvular heart disease
questions.explanation: Primary or essential hypertension accounts for 90-95% of cases with no identifiable secondary cause. Secondary causes like kidney disease account for only 5-10% of cases.

questions.q: Sepsis is defined as a systemic response to infection characterized by what?
options: Any fever in a hospitalized patient
Life-threatening organ dysfunction caused by dysregulated host response to infection
Presence of bacteria in the bloodstream
Antibiotic-resistant bacterial infection
questions.explanation: Sepsis represents organ dysfunction from dysregulated immune response, not simply the presence of infection or fever. Bacteremia alone (bacteria in blood) doesn't define sepsis.

questions.q: Which mechanism explains how metastasis occurs in cancer?
options: Cancer cells divide faster than normal cells
Cancer cells lose adhesion, invade tissue, intravasate, survive in circulation, and extravasate at distant sites
Cancer cells simply divide larger in size
Tumors expand outward in a spherical pattern
questions.explanation: Metastasis involves a multi-step process: epithelial-mesenchymal transition, invasion, intravasation into blood vessels, survival in circulation, extravasation, and colonization of distant tissue. Speed of division alone does not cause metastasis.

questions.q: Osteoporosis is characterized by what structural bone change?
options: Increased bone mineral density
Decreased bone mineral density with architectural deterioration
Thickening of the cortical bone layer
Increased osteoid formation without mineralization
questions.explanation: Osteoporosis involves both decreased bone mineral density and deterioration of bone microarchitecture, increasing fracture risk. Osteomalacia (the option about unmineralized osteoid) is a different condition.
